items.q: Why was the founding of the NHS controversial in 1948?
items.correct: The British Medical Association opposed nationalisation of doctors; Bevan made concessions allowing private practice; Conservatives feared cost and central control.
items.misconception: The NHS was supported by all parties and all doctors from the start.
items.random_wrong: The NHS was originally only for soldiers returning from WWII.
items.why: Bevan said he had to 'stuff their mouths with gold' to get doctors on board. Students project today's cross-party support backwards onto a deeply contested founding.

items.q: Why did the Treaty of Versailles cause resentment in Germany?
items.correct: It assigned sole war guilt to Germany (Article 231) and imposed harsh reparations, territorial losses and military restrictions.
items.misconception: Because Germany lost more soldiers than any other country in the war.
items.random_wrong: Because Germany was not invited to sign it.
items.why: Students conflate the harshness of the treaty with battlefield losses. Germany WAS excluded from negotiations, but the resentment was about the terms (especially the war-guilt clause) — not exclusion from signing.

items.q: Did the Treaty of Versailles directly cause WWII?
items.correct: It contributed via German resentment and economic strain, but the 1930s Depression, Hitler's choices, and Allied appeasement were equally necessary — Versailles alone is not sufficient.
items.misconception: Versailles caused WWII directly because it was so harsh on Germany.
items.random_wrong: Versailles caused WWII because it required Germany to invade Poland.
items.why: Comparable peace terms have not always led to war. Modern historians stress contingency; treating Versailles as sufficient cause is a simple but inadequate story.

items.q: What carried the fleas that spread plague?
items.why: It's Black rats. “Cats” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: A black swelling on the body
items.why: It's A black swelling on the body. “A type of rat” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did the Black Death first arrive in England?
items.why: The real figure is 1348. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
